subject: Ecology
title: Pollination and Food Security
description: A 60-second lesson on Pollination and Food Security.

hook.text: One in every three bites of food you eat exists because a pollinator visited a flower.

buildup.text: Apples, almonds, coffee, chocolate, and tomatoes all depend on animal pollination.

discovery.text: Pollinators contribute an estimated $235 billion to global agriculture each year.

twist.text: Many staple grains—wheat, rice, corn—are wind-pollinated, so they don't need bees.

climax.text: Losing pollinators wouldn't end farming, but it would strip diets of most fruits and vegetables.

punchline.text: Pollinators don't just feed bees. They feed us.

quiz.question: How much do pollinators contribute to global agriculture annually?
